behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: Minimum boundary is the real embedding-model call plus index contract. A stubbed embedder can mask wrong model selection/version drift that changes vector size. I considered Integration, but rejected it because the behavior explicitly says "embedding vector produced" (real model output shape), not just local adapter formatting. I considered Workflow and rejected it because this is a single model-dependent check, not a multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model with representative queries, read index dimension from the real vector DB schema/config, and assert returned vector length equals index dimension and can be accepted by the DB write/search path.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is vector-search engine behavior (metric config, ordering, tie handling) over real DB/API execution. In-process mocks often return ideal sorted fixtures and hide engine/config drift. I considered Integration, but rejected it because this behavior is about actual search ordering from the vector DB, which depends on real infrastructure behavior. I considered Agentic and rejected it because the assertion is on cosine-score ordering logic, not model output quality; fixed query vectors can be used.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real vector DB instance/API, load a controlled dataset with known vectors, run search via production search path, and assert returned scores are monotonic non-increasing and ranking matches cosine expectations.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: This directly tests model judgment on contextual relevance, which cannot be validated by deterministic stubs. Mocking would hide the exact failure mode (LLM fails to reinterpret nuance). I considered Workflow, but rejected it because the behavior targets one model decision stage (re-rank), not multiple agentic decisions chained with separate correctness gates.
behaviors.test_description: Use real LLM re-ranking with curated nuanced queries and candidate lists where raw similarity is known to be suboptimal; assert at least one top-N position changes and the revised order better matches expected intent criteria.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: The pass/fail condition depends on semantic closeness from the embedding model (and potentially retrieval semantics), so using fixed stubs can miss real false-positive neighbors. I considered Integration, but rejected it because the behavior is about relevance/noise outcome, not only plumbing. I considered Workflow and rejected it because this does not require validating multiple agentic decisions in sequence; one model-dependent retrieval boundary is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run with real embeddings and real retrieval on a corpus where specific out-of-domain queries are expected to have no semantic neighbors above threshold; assert API returns empty results (not low-score filler) consistently under defined cutoff policy.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end SLO across multiple sequential real steps, including embedding and LLM re-ranking calls. Mocking either step hides true latency distribution and interaction effects. I considered System, but rejected it because behavior text is explicitly full pipeline: "from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results," which includes multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like load test through the real API path with real embedding + vector search + real LLM re-rank, collect latency distribution, and assert median (p50) stays under 2.0s for the defined median-case workload.
